title: Two Features - 1. PDF Creation from Items and/or Subitems | 2. HTML Support for Email Integrations
excerpt:
1. PDF Creation from Items and/or Subitems 
Specifically inline creation of PDFs(or other supported documents) from items and/or subitems. I’ve tried three other Apps in the marketplace and they all miss the mark for my …

posts.author: andrewalmand (Andrew Almand)
posts.cookedHtml:
1. PDF Creation from Items and/or Subitems
Specifically inline creation of PDFs(or other supported documents) from items and/or subitems. I’ve tried three other Apps in the marketplace and they all miss the mark for my companies use. The crux is either complicated template creation, unsupported Monday columns/placeholders for templates or not integrating easily on an existing Monday board. I would like know where Monday.com is with their own built in PDF creation.

HTML Support for Email Integrations
The ability to send emails from integrations is great but currently we can only use it as automated notification system for reps outside our organization. But with only plain text, no way to included CC or BCC, add attachments, proper signatures, links etc., we cannot use it for more complicated emails. I would love to send quote request/orders/estimates, schedule shipments, send documents(PDFS generated by the other feature?!) but cannot until Monday natievely includes HTML for email integrations. I know of and used integrations like Integromat but the triggers for setting it up makes it more complicated than I would like. Its an okay solution for SOME use-cases in the meantime. But again I would like to know when Monday plans to have this feature built-in.

posts.author: JacksonBarry (Jackson Barry)
posts.cookedHtml: @andrewalmand have you found a solution or heard from Monday.com? I’ve tried Docugen, Eledo, and Docative, and am getting by with them, but I agree with you about the frustrations. I’d really like to see a user-friendly PDF generator with a polished looking final product native to monday.com.

posts.author: eClaireS (Claire Sowden)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @JacksonBarry,

We are also interested in a solution like this.
We use items for tasks that need to be actioned by a volunteer disaster response team, who do not have Monday access. Typically, these teams are best suited to a piece of paper with the job details.

This means we need a custom template with job details and an area for the volunteer to complete job notes that will be entered back into Monday.

I’ve had a quick look at Docotive & DocuGen but I feel they lack a custom template option that would allow the written feedback area.

Is that something you have found as well? (or should I keep looking)

posts.author: JHconsultancy (Joseph Honig)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

You can use make.com for this.

DM if you need further assistance

posts.author: JenefaJ (Jenefa)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, this is possible with The PDF Maker (app.thepdfmaker.com).

The PDF Maker allows you to create PDFs from both items and subitems in Monday.com with a simple drag-and-drop template builder. You can map values from any Monday column—including custom fields—into your document, and automate PDF generation based on triggers like a status change or form submission.

This means you won’t need complex template setups or struggle with integration barriers.

Every new response triggers PDF generation automatically.

Please view the support article below:

help.thepdfmaker.com

Automate Documents with the Monday App - Full Guide | The PDF Maker Help Center

Master document automation using the Monday app and PDF Maker. Streamline workflows and enhance productivity by effortlessly generating PDFs directly from Monday.com.

posts.author: Flowtech (Flowtech Apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
If you’re looking for an easy way to create customized documents (like PDFs, invoices, quotes, reports, or contracts) directly from your monday.com data, you might want to check out DocCreate.

With DocCreate, you can:

  • Automatically generate documents using your board data
  • Customize layouts with your branding, logos, headers, and footers
  • Embed images directly from your boards for richer, more visual documents
  • Pull data from multiple columns, connected boards, or subitems
  • Save generated documents directly into file columns on your boards
  • Automate document creation triggered by item updates or form submissions
  • Send the generated document automatically via email to clients or stakeholders

It’s a great way to eliminate manual exports, double entry, and formatting headaches — all within your monday workflow.

posts.author: AshcroftJewellery (Christian Fox )
posts.cookedHtml:
It would be grand if extremely old posts weren’t bumped by salespeople! This has not been relevant for 2 years!

I understand the need for pushing a product, but it would be wonderful if these were on recent posts! Especially when the sales person is bumping multiple threads about the same topic!

My topic has no been bumped off the front page and I’m still waiting for responses! This makes it difficult to engage with recent/relevant posts.

Cheers
